REPLY IN SUPPORT OF MOTION OF DANIEL CASABONNE FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS (TRANSACTION ID # 58763088) FILED BY DEFENDANT CASABONNE, DANIEL

LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT DANIEL CASABONNE'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS IS DENIED. BASED ON THE STANDARD FOR A MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS, THE COURT FINDS THAT PLAINTIFF HAS IMPLICITLY ALLEGED THAT DEFENDANT'S ACCEPTING THE REFERRAL IS SYNONYMOUS WITH AN EXECUTION OF THE AGREEMENT. THE COURT CANNOT FIND AT THIS STAGE THAT THAT IS NOT REASONABLE. PLAINTIFF SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT FOR SIGNATURE WHICH MAY BE E-MAILED TO THE COURT AT CONTESTDEPT302TR@SFTC.ORG. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Nov-10-2016 Vacated

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-28-2016 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-05-2016 Set For Trial On Dec-05-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Daniel Casabonne For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Mar-30-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Attys. JONATHAN CARDELLA, ESQ. (Tel No. 612-339-8131) and ADAM P. GISLASON, ESQ. (Tel No. 612-336-9351) of Lommen, Abdo, Cole, King & Stageberg appearing telephonically for the Plaintiff;
Atty. JILL K. COHOE, ESQ. of Manning & Kass Ellrod, Ramirez, Trester LLP, Tel No. 415-217-6990 appearing for the Defendant Daniel Casabonne.

The Court makes a disclosure regarding its prior professional relationship with attorney Jill Cohoe, and that it finds no need to disqualify itself. Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court vacates its tentative ruling, and orders as follows: DEFENDANT DANIEL CASABONNE'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings is denied. Based on the standard for a motion for judgment on the pleadings, the Court finds that Plaintiff has implicitly alleged that Defendant's accepting the referral is synonymous with an execution of the agreement. The Court cannot find at this stage that that is not reasonable. Plaintiff shall prepare and submit a proposed order to the Court for signature which may be e-mailed to the court at contestdept302tr@sftc.org. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Mar-09-2016 Continued To May-18-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-03-2015 Continued To Nov-18-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-21-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT DANIEL CASABONNE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend. Pursuant to CCP 430.10(e), the court will sustain Defendant's demurrer with ten days leave to amend on the grounds that Plaintiff has failed to plead facts which sufficiently establish the existence of a valid contract between the parties. The terms of the Referral Agreement required execution by Defendant. Neither the case law cited by Plaintiff, nor the references to the Uniform Electronic Transactions Act CC 1633.1 et seq. support Plaintiff's position that the typed words, "accepted by telephone," constitute an electronic signature. Because Plaintiff has not pled facts that Defendant executed the agreement, Plaintiff has failed to state facts sufficient enough to bring a cause of action for breach of contract. Order signed. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-05-2015 Continued To Sep-21-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
